verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin exists in two major forms: a soluble plasma form (pFN, dimeric, secreted into circulation) and a cellular/matrix form (cFN, multimeric) assembled at the cell surface and incorporated into ECM fibrils. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is 'present in a dimeric or multimeric form at the cell surface and in extracellular matrix.' Cell-surface fibronectin is detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact, non-permeabilized cells, where it is anchored via integrin interactions and fibronectin-fibronectin self-assembly rather than a transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. Because the dominant pool is secreted/matrix-deposited and surface display depends on cell-type context (fibroblasts, endothelial cells, tumor cells) and assembly state, this best fits 'dual_localization' — a documented PM-associated pool alongside a dominant non-PM (secreted/circulating/ECM) compartment. Anti-FN antibodies and FN-targeting therapeutics (e.g., targeting the ED-B isoform in tumor vasculature with antibody-drug conjugates and radioligand therapies) exploit the cell-surface/matrix-associated pool, confirming surface accessibility in relevant contexts.
